$\Leftrightarrow R^2 = 625$
$\Leftrightarrow R = 25 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: 66 cubic centimetres of silver is drawn into a wire 1 mm in diameter. The length of the wire in metres will be
questions.solution:
Let the length of the wire be $h$. Radius = $\frac{1}{2} \text{ mm} = \frac{1}{20} \text{ cm}$. Then,
$\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{1}{20} \times \frac{1}{20} \times h = 66$
$\Leftrightarrow h = \left(\frac{66 \times 20 \times 20 \times 7}{22}\right) = 8400 \text{ cm} = 84 \text{ m}$.

questions.question_text: A copper rod of 1 cm diameter and 8 cm length is drawn into a wire of uniform diameter and 18 m length. The radius (in cm) of the wire is,
questions.solution:
Volume of copper rod = $\left(\pi \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times 8\right) \text{cm}^3 = 2\pi \text{cm}^3$.
Let the radius of the wire be $r \text{ cm}$.
Then, volume of wire = $(\pi r^2 \times 1800) \text{cm}^3 = 1800 \pi r^2 \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore 1800 \pi r^2 = 2\pi$
$\Leftrightarrow r^2 = \frac{2}{1800} = \frac{1}{900}$
$\Leftrightarrow r = \sqrt{\frac{1}{900}} = \frac{1}{30}$.

questions.passage_text: The diameter of a garden roller is 1.4 m and it is 2 m long. How much area will it cover in 5 revolutions?
questions.question_text: The diameter of a garden roller is 1.4 m and it is 2 m long. How much area will it cover in 5 revolutions?
questions.solution:
Curved surface area of the roller
$= \left(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 0.7 \times 2\right) \text{m}^2 = 8.8 \text{ m}^2$.
$\therefore$ Area covered in 5 revolutions = $(8.8 \times 5) \text{ m}^2 = 44 \text{ m}^2$.

questions.question_text: A square pond has 2 m sides and is 1 m deep. If it is to be enlarged, the depth remaining the same, into a circular pond with the diagonal of the square as diameter as shown in the figure, then what would be the volume of earth to be removed?
questions.solution:
Diagonal of the square = $\sqrt{2^2 + 2^2} \text{ m} = \sqrt{8} \text{ m} = 2\sqrt{2} \text{ m}$.
Diameter of circular pond = $2\sqrt{2} \text{ m}$. Radius of circular pond = $\sqrt{2} \text{ m}$.
Volume of circular pond = $\left[\pi \times (\sqrt{2})^2 \times 1\right] \text{m}^3 = 2\pi \text{m}^3$.
Volume of square pond = $(2 \times 2 \times 1) \text{m}^3 = 4 \text{m}^3$.
$\therefore$ Volume of earth to be removed = $(2\pi - 4) \text{m}^3$.

questions.question_text: What part of a ditch, 48 metres long, 16.5 metres broad and 4 metres deep can be filled by the earth got by digging a cylindrical tunnel of diameter 4 metres and length 56 metres?
questions.solution:
Volume of earth dug = $\left(\frac{22}{7} \times 2 \times 2 \times 56\right) \text{m}^3 = 704 \text{m}^3$.
Volume of ditch = $(48 \times 16.5 \times 4) \text{m}^3 = 3168 \text{m}^3$.
$\therefore$ Required fraction = $\frac{704}{3168} = \frac{2}{9}$.

questions.question_text: Water is flowing at the rate of 5 km/hr through a cylindrical pipe of diameter 14 cm into a rectangular tank which is 50 m long and 44 m wide. Determine the time in which the level of water in the tank will rise by 7 cm.
questions.solution:
Volume of water flown into the tank
$= (50 \times 44 \times 0.07) \text{m}^3 = 154 \text{m}^3$.
Volume of water flowing through the pipe in 1 hour
$= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 0.07 \times 0.07 \times 5000\right) \text{m}^3 = 77 \text{m}^3$.
$\therefore$ Required time = $\left(\frac{154}{77}\right) = 2 \text{ hrs}$.

questions.question_text: The trunk of a tree is a right cylinder 1.5 m in radius and 10 m high. The volume of the timber which remains when the trunk is trimmed just enough to reduce it to a rectangular parallelopiped on a square base is
questions.solution:
Let the length of each side of the square base be $x$ metres.
Then, $x^2 + x^2 = 32 \Rightarrow 2x^2 = 9$
$\Rightarrow x^2 = \frac{9}{2} \Rightarrow x = \frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}$.
$\therefore$ Volume of parallelopiped
$= \left(\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} \times 10\right) \text{m}^3 = \frac{90}{2} \text{m}^3 = 45 \text{m}^3$.

questions.question_text: Rain water, which falls on a flat rectangular surface of length 6 m and breadth 4 m is transferred into a cylindrical vessel of internal radius 20 cm. What will be the height of water in the cylindrical vessel if a rainfall of 1 cm has fallen?
questions.solution:
Volume of rain water = $(600 \times 400 \times 1) \text{cm}^3 = 240000 \text{cm}^3$.
Let the height of water in the cylindrical vessel be $h \text{ cm}$.
Then, $\frac{22}{7} \times 20 \times 20 \times h = 240000 \Rightarrow h = \frac{240000 \times 7}{22 \times 20 \times 20} = \frac{2100}{11}$
$= 190.9 \text{ cm} \approx 191 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: An iron pipe 20 cm long has exterior diameter equal to 25 cm. If the thickness of the pipe is 1 cm, then the whole surface of the pipe is
questions.solution:
External radius, $R = \frac{25}{2} \text{ cm}$.
Internal radius, $r = \left(\frac{25}{2} - 1\right) \text{ cm} = \frac{23}{2} \text{ cm}$.
Length, $h = 20 \text{ cm}$.
Whole surface area = $2\pi Rh + 2\pi rh + 2\pi (R^2 - r^2) = 2\pi [(R + r) h + (R^2 - r^2)]$
$= 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times \left[\left(\frac{25}{2} + \frac{23}{2}\right) \times 20 + \left(\frac{25}{2} + \frac{23}{2}\right) \left(\frac{25}{2} - \frac{23}{2}\right)\right]$
$= 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times [24 \times 20 + 24 \times 1] \text{cm}^2 = 3168 \text{cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: A hollow garden roller 63 cm wide with a girth of 440 cm is made of iron 4 cm thick. The volume of the iron used is
questions.solution:
Circumference of the girth = $440 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore 2\pi R = 440 \Rightarrow R = \left(440 \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{7}{22}\right) = 70 \text{ cm}$.
So, Outer radius = $70 \text{ cm}$.
Inner radius = $(70 - 4) \text{ cm} = 66 \text{ cm}$.
Volume of iron = $\pi [(70)^2 - (66)^2] \times 63$
$= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 136 \times 4 \times 63\right) \text{cm}^3 = 58752 \text{cm}^3$.

questions.question_text: A cylindrical tube open at both ends is made of metal. The internal diameter of the tube is 11.2 cm and its length is 21 cm. The metal everywhere is 0.4 cm thick. The volume of the metal is
questions.solution:
Internal radius = $\frac{11.2}{2} \text{ cm} = 5.6 \text{ cm}$.
External radius = $(5.6 + 0.4) \text{ cm} = 6 \text{ cm}$.
Volume of metal
$= \left\{\frac{22}{7} \times [(6)^2 - (5.6)^2] \times 21\right\} \text{cm}^3 = (66 \times 11.6 \times 0.4) \text{cm}^3$
$= 306.24 \text{cm}^3$.

questions.question_text: What length of solid cylinder 2 cm in diameter must be taken to cast into a hollow cylinder of external diameter 12 cm, 0.25 cm thick and 15 cm long?
questions.solution:
External radius = $6 \text{ cm}$,
Internal radius = $(6 - 0.25) \text{ cm} = 5.75 \text{ cm}$.
Volume of material in hollow cylinder
$= \left\{\frac{22}{7} \times [(6)^2 - (5.75)^2] \times 15\right\} \text{cm}^3$
$= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 11.75 \times 0.25 \times 15\right) \text{cm}^3 = \left(\frac{22}{7} \times \frac{1175}{100} \times \frac{25}{100} \times 15\right) \text{cm}^3 = \frac{11705}{56} \text{cm}^3$.
Let the length of solid cylinder be $h$. Then,
$\frac{22}{7} \times 1 \times 1 \times h = \frac{11705}{56} \Leftrightarrow h = \left(\frac{11705}{56} \times \frac{7}{22}\right) \text{cm} = 44.0625 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: A hollow iron pipe is 21 cm long and its external diameter is 8 cm. If the thickness of the pipe is 1 cm and iron weighs 8 g/cm$^3$, then the weight of the pipe is
questions.solution:
External radius = $4 \text{ cm}$, Internal radius = $3 \text{ cm}$.
Volume of iron
$= \left\{\frac{22}{7} \times [(4)^2 - (3)^2] \times 21\right\} \text{cm}^3 = \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 21\right) \text{cm}^3 = 462 \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore$ Weight of iron = $(462 \times 8) \text{ gm} = 3696 \text{ gm} = 3.696 \text{ kg}$.

questions.question_text: 1496 cm$^3$ of metal is used to cast a pipe of length 28 cm. If the internal radius of the pipe is 8 cm, the outer radius of the pipe is
questions.solution:
Let the outer radius of the pipe be $R \text{ cm}$.
Then, volume of metal used = External volume - Internal volume = $\pi R^2 h - \pi r^2 h = \pi h (R^2 - r^2)$
$= \frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times (R^2 - 8^2) = 88(R^2 - 64)$.
$\Rightarrow 88(R^2 - 64) = 1496 \Rightarrow R^2 - 64 = 17 \Rightarrow R^2 = 81 \Rightarrow R = 9 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: A milkman saves milk in two vessels, a cuboidal and the other a cylindrical one. The capacity of the cuboidal vessel is 20 litres more than the cylinddrical one. When 30 litres of milk is drawn from each of the two full vessels, the amount left in the cuboidal vessel is twice that left in the cylindrical vessel. The capacity (in litres) of the cuboidal vessel is
questions.solution:
Let the capacity of the cylindrical vessel be $x$ litres.
Then, capacity of the cuboidal vessel = $(x + 20)$ litres.
$\therefore (x + 20) - 30 = 2(x - 30) \Rightarrow x - 10 = 2x - 60 \Rightarrow x = 50$.

questions.question_text: A circular cylinder can hold 61.6 c.c. of water. If the height of the cylinder is 40 cm and the outer diameter is 16 mm, then the thickness of the material of the cylinder is
questions.solution:
Let the internal radius of the cylinder be $x$. Then,
$\frac{22}{7} \times x^2 \times 40 = 616 \Leftrightarrow x^2 = \left(\frac{616 \times 7}{22 \times 40}\right) = 0.49 \Leftrightarrow x = 0.7$.
So, internal radius = $0.7 \text{ cm} = 7 \text{ mm}$.
$\therefore$ Thickness = $(8 - 7) \text{ mm} = 1 \text{ mm}$.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following figures will generate a cone when rotated about one of its straight edges?
questions.solution: Not provided in the source text.

questions.question_text: The radius of the base and height of a cone are 3 cm and 5 cm respectively whereas the radius of the base and height of a cylinder are 2 cm and 4 cm respectively. The ratio of the volume of cone to that of the cylinder is
questions.solution: $\frac{\text{Volume of cone}}{\text{Volume of cylinder}} = \frac{\frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times (3)^2 \times 5}{\pi \times (2)^2 \times 4} = \frac{45}{48} = \frac{15}{16}$.

questions.question_text: What is the weight of water contained in a conical vessel 21 cm deep and 16 cm in diameter?
questions.solution:
Volume of water = $\left(\frac{1}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times8\times8\times21\right) \text{ cm}^3 = 1408 \text{ cm}^3$
$= \frac{1408}{1000} \text{ kg} = 1.408 \text{ kg}$.

questions.question_text: Find the slant height of the cone whose height is 4.8 cm and the diameter of base is 4 cm.
questions.solution:
Radius, $r = 2 \text{ cm}$. Height, $h = 4.8 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Slant height, $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{2^2 + (4.8)^2} \text{ cm}$
$= \sqrt{4 + 23.04} \text{ cm} = \sqrt{27.04} \text{ cm} = 5.2 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: The curved surface of a right circular cone of height 84 cm and base diameter 70 cm is
questions.solution:
$h = 84 \text{ cm}$, $r = 35 \text{ cm}$.
So, $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{(35)^2 + (84)^2} = \sqrt{8281} \text{ cm} = 91 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area
$= \pi rl = \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 35 \times 91\right) \text{ cm}^2 = 10010 \text{ cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: The curved surface of a right circular cone of height 15 cm and base diameter 16 cm is
questions.solution:
$h = 15 \text{ cm}$, $r = 8 \text{ cm}$. So, $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{8^2 + (15)^2} = 17 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area $= \pi rl = (\pi \times 8 \times 17) \text{ cm}^2$
$= 136\pi \text{ cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: What is the total surface area of a right circular cone of height 14 cm and base radius 7 cm?
questions.solution:
$h = 14 \text{ cm}$, $r = 7 \text{ cm}$. So, $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{(7)^2 + (14)^2} = \sqrt{245} = 7\sqrt{5} \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Total surface area $= \pi rl + \pi r^2$
$= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7\sqrt{5} + \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7\right) \text{ cm}^2$

questions.question_text: A right triangle with sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm is rotated about the side of 3 cm to form a cone. The volume of the cone so formed is
questions.solution:
Clearly, we have $r = 3 \text{ cm}$ and $h = 4 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Volume $= \left(\frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 3^2 \times 4\right) \text{ cm}^3 = 12\pi \text{ cm}^3$.

questions.question_text: The slant height of a right circular cone is 10 m and its height is 8 m. Find the area of its curved surface.
questions.solution:
$l = 10 \text{ m}$, $h = 8 \text{ m}$. So, $r = \sqrt{l^2 - h^2} = \sqrt{(10)^2 - 8^2} = 6 \text{ m}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area $= \pi rl = (\pi \times 6 \times 10) \text{ m}^2 = 60\pi \text{ m}^2$.

questions.question_text: If a right circular cone of height 24 cm has a volume of 1232 $cm^3$, then the area of its curved surface is
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^2 \times 24 = 1232 \Rightarrow r^2 = \frac{1232 \times 7 \times 3}{22 \times 24} = 49$
$\Leftrightarrow r = 7$.
Now, $r = 7 \text{ cm}$, $h = 24 \text{ cm}$. So, $l = \sqrt{(7)^2 + (24)^2} = 25 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area $= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 25\right) \text{ cm}^2 = 550 \text{ cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: A conical tent is to accommodate 11 persons. Each person must have 4 sq. metres of the space on the ground and 20 cubic metres of air to breathe. The height of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let radius of base $= r$ and height $= h$.
Required floor area $= (4 \times 11) \text{ m}^2 = 44 \text{ m}^2$. So, $\pi r^2 = 44$.
Required volume $= (20 \times 11) \text{ m}^3 = 220 \text{ m}^3$.
So, $\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h = 220 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \times 44 \times h = 220 \Rightarrow h = \frac{220 \times 3}{44} = 15 \text{ m}$.

questions.question_text: Area of the canvas cloth needed to erect a right conical tent of height 12 ft and circular base having circumference 10π ft is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of base be $r \text{ ft}$. Then, $2\pi r = 10\pi$ or $r = 5$.
$l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{5^2 + (12)^2} = \sqrt{169} = 13 \text{ ft}$.
$\therefore$ Area of cloth $= \pi rl = (\pi \times 5 \times 13) \text{ sq ft} = 65\pi \text{ sq ft}$.

questions.question_text: The slant height of a conical mountain is 2.5 km and the area of its base is 1.54 $km^2$. The height of the mountain is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the base be $r \text{ km}$. Then,
$\pi r^2 = 1.54 \Rightarrow r^2 = \frac{1.54 \times 7}{22} = 0.49 \Rightarrow r = 0.7 \text{ km}$.
Now, $l = 2.5 \text{ km}$, $r = 0.7 \text{ km}$.
$\therefore h = \sqrt{l^2 - r^2} = \sqrt{(2.5)^2 - (0.7)^2} \text{ km} = \sqrt{6.25 - 0.49} \text{ km} = \sqrt{5.76} \text{ km} = 2.4 \text{ km}$.
So, height of the mountain $= 2.4 \text{ km}$.

questions.question_text: If the area of the base of a right circular cone is 3850 $cm^2$ and its height is 84 cm, then the curved surface area of the cone is
questions.solution:
$\pi r^2 = 3850 \Rightarrow r^2 = \frac{3850 \times 7}{22} = 1225 \Rightarrow r = 35$.
Now, $r = 35 \text{ cm}$, $h = 84 \text{ cm}$.
So, $l = \sqrt{(35)^2 + (84)^2} = \sqrt{1225 + 7056} = \sqrt{8281} = 91 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area $= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 35 \times 91\right) \text{ cm}^2 = 10010 \text{ cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: Volume of a right circular cone having base radius 70 cm and curved surface area 40040 $cm^2$ is
questions.solution:
$\frac{22}{7} \times 70 \times l = 40040 \Rightarrow l = \frac{40040 \times 7}{22 \times 70} = 182$.
Now, $l = 182 \text{ cm}$, $r = 70 \text{ cm}$.
So, $h = \sqrt{(182)^2 - (70)^2} = \sqrt{252 \times 112} = 168 \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore$ Volume $= \left(\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 70 \times 70 \times 168\right) \text{ cm}^3 = 862400 \text{ cm}^3$.

questions.question_text: The radius and height of a right circular cone are in the ratio 3 : 4. If its volume is $301 rac{5}{7} cm^3$, what is its slant height?
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the cone be $3x$ and $4x$ respectively. Then,
$\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times (3x)^2 \times 4x = \frac{2112}{7} \Rightarrow \frac{264}{7} x^3 = \frac{2112}{7} \Rightarrow x^3 = 8 \Leftrightarrow x = 2$.
$\therefore$ Radius $= 6 \text{ cm}$, Height $= 8 \text{ cm}$. Slant height $= \sqrt{6^2 + 8^2} \text{ cm} = \sqrt{100} \text{ cm} = 10 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: A vertical cone of volume V with vertex downwards is filled with water upto half of its height. The volume of the water is
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the cone be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, $V = \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h$.
Now, $\Delta AOB \sim \Delta COD$.
So, $\frac{OA}{OC} = \frac{AB}{CD} \Rightarrow \frac{h}{h/2} = \frac{r}{CD} \Rightarrow CD = \frac{r}{2}$.
$\therefore$ Volume of water $= \frac{1}{3} \pi \left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^2 \left(\frac{h}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{8} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right) = \frac{V}{8}$.

questions.question_text: A semicircular sheet of paper of diameter 28 cm is bent to cover the exterior surface of an open conical ice-cream cup. The depth of the ice-cream cup is
questions.solution:
Slant height of the cup, $l = \text{Radius of sheet} = 14 \text{ cm}$.
Circumference of the base $= \text{Circumference of the paper sheet} = \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 14\right) \text{ cm} = 44 \text{ cm}$.
Let the radius of the base of the cone be $r \text{ cm}$.
$\therefore 2\pi r = 44 \Rightarrow r = \frac{44 \times 7}{2 \times 22} = 7$.
Height, $h = \sqrt{l^2 - r^2} = \sqrt{(14)^2 - 7^2} = \sqrt{147} = 7\sqrt{3} \text{ cm} = 12.12 \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: The length of canvas 1.1 m wide required to build a conical tent of height 14 m and the floor area 346.5 sq. m is
questions.solution:
$\pi r^2 = 346.5 \Rightarrow r^2 = 346.5 \times \frac{7}{22} = 110.25 \Rightarrow r = 10.5$.
$\therefore l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{(10.5)^2 + (14)^2} = \sqrt{110.25 + 196} = \sqrt{306.25} = 17.5$.
So, area of canvas needed $= \pi rl = \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 10.5 \times 17.5\right) \text{ m}^2 = 577.5 \text{ m}^2$.
$\therefore$ Length of canvas $= \frac{577.5}{1.1} \text{ m} = 525 \text{ m}$.

questions.question_text: If the height of a cone is doubled and its base diameter is trebled, then the ratio of the volume of the resultant cone to that of the original cone is
questions.solution:
Let the original radius and height of the cone be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, new radius $= 3r$ and new height $= 2h$.
$\therefore \frac{\text{New volume}}{\text{Original volume}} = \frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi (3r)^2 \times 2h}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h} = \frac{18}{1}$.

questions.question_text: If both the radius and height of a right circular cone are increased by 20%, its volume will be increased by
questions.solution:
Let the original radius and height of the cone be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, Original volume $= \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h$.
New radius $= \frac{120}{100} r = \frac{6}{5} r$, New height $= \frac{6}{5} h$.
New volume $= \frac{1}{3} \pi \left(\frac{6}{5} r\right)^2 \left(\frac{6}{5} h\right) = \frac{216}{125} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right)$.
Increase in volume $= \frac{91}{125} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right)$.
$\therefore$ Increase $\% = \left(\frac{91}{125} \times 100\right) \% = 72.8 \%$.

questions.question_text: If the height of a right circular cone is increased by 200% and the radius of the base is reduced by 50%, then the volume of the cone
questions.solution:
Let the original radius and height of the cone be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, original volume $= \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h$.

questions.question_text: If the height of a cone be doubled and radius of base remains the same, then the ratio of the volume of the given cone to that of the second cone will be
questions.solution: Required ratio = $\frac{\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h}{\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 (2h)} = \frac{1}{2}$.

questions.question_text: If the height, curved surface area and the volume of a cone are h, c and v respectively, then $3	ext{π} vh^3 - c^2h^2 + 9v^2$ will be equal to
questions.solution:
Volume of the cone, $v = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.
Curved surface area, $c = \pi rl = \pi r \sqrt{r^2 + h^2}$
$\Rightarrow c^2 = \pi^2 r^2 (r^2 + h^2)$.
$\therefore 3\pi v h^3 - c^2 h^2 + 9v^2$
$= 3\pi \left(\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h\right) h^3 - \pi^2 r^2 (r^2 + h^2) h^2 + 9 \left(\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h\right)^2$
$= \pi^2 r^2 h^4 - \pi^2 r^4 h^2 - \pi^2 r^2 h^4 + \pi^2 r^4 h^2 = 0$.

questions.question_text: If the heights of two cones are in the ratio 7 : 3 and their diameters are in the ratio 6 : 7, what is the ratio of their volumes?
questions.solution:
Let the heights of two cones be $7x$ and $3x$ and their radii be $6y$ and $7y$ respectively. Then,
Ratio of volumes = $\frac{\frac{1}{3}\pi (6y)^2 \times 7x}{\frac{1}{3}\pi (7y)^2 \times 3x} = \frac{36 \times 7}{49 \times 3} = \frac{12}{7}$.

questions.question_text: The radii of two cones are in the ratio 2 : 1, their volumes are equal. Find the ratio of their heights.
questions.solution:
Let their radii be $2x, x$ and their heights be $h$ and $H$ respectively. Then,
$\frac{1}{3}\pi (2x)^2 \times h = \frac{1}{3}\pi x^2 \times H \Rightarrow \frac{h}{H} = \frac{1}{4}$.

questions.question_text: If the ratio of volumes of two cones is 2 : 3 and the ratio of the radii of their bases is 1 : 2, then the ratio of their heights will be
questions.solution:
Let their radii be $x$ and $2x$, and their heights be $h$ and $H$ respectively. Then,
$\frac{\frac{1}{3}\pi x^2 \times h}{\frac{1}{3}\pi (2x)^2 \times H} = \frac{2}{3}$ or $\frac{h}{H} = \frac{8}{3}$.

questions.question_text: Find the volume of the largest right circular cone that can be cut out from a cube whose edge is 9 cm.
questions.solution:
Volume of the largest cone
= Volume of the cone with diameter of base $9$ cm and height $9$ cm.
$= \left(\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 4.5 \times 4.5 \times 9\right) \text{cm}^3 = \left(\frac{1336.5}{7}\right) \text{cm}^3 = 190.93 \text{cm}^3$.

questions.question_text: A cone of height 7 cm and base radius 3 cm is carved from a rectangular block of wood 10 cm $	imes$ 5 cm $	imes$ 2 cm. The percentage of wood wasted is
questions.solution:
Volume of the block = $(10 \times 5 \times 2) \text{cm}^3 = 100 \text{cm}^3$.
Volume of the cone carved out =
$\left(\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times 3 \times 7\right) \text{cm}^3 = 66 \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore$ Wood wasted = $(100 - 66)\% = 34\%$.

questions.question_text: A right circular cone and a right circular cylinder have equal base and equal height. If the radius of the base and the height are in the ratio 5 : 12, then the ratio of the total surface area of the cylinder to that of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let their radius and height be $5x$ and $12x$ respectively.
Slant height of the cone, $l = \sqrt{(5x)^2 + (12x)^2} = 13x$.
$\frac{\text{Total surface area of cylinder}}{\text{Total surface area of cone}} = \frac{2\pi r(h+r)}{\pi r(l+r)}$
$= \frac{2(12x + 5x)}{(13x + 5x)} = \frac{34x}{18x} = \frac{17}{9}$.

questions.question_text: A conical cavity is drilled in a circular cylinder of 15 cm height and 16 cm base diameter. The height and base diameter of the cone are the same as those of the cylinder. Determine the total surface area of the remaining solid.
questions.solution:
Total surface area of the remaining solid = Curved surface area of the cylinder + Area of the base of the cylinder + Curved surface area of the cone
$= 2\pi rh + \pi r^2 + \pi rl$
$= 2\pi \times 8 \times 15 + \pi \times 8^2 + \pi \times 8 \times \sqrt{8^2 + 15^2}$
$= 240\pi + 64\pi + 136\pi = 440\pi \text{sq. cm}$.

questions.question_text: The radius of the base and height of a metallic solid cylinder are r cm and 6 cm respectively. It is melted and recast into a solid cone of the same radius of base. The height of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cone be $h$ cm.
Then, $\pi \times r^2 \times 6 = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times r^2 \times h \Rightarrow h = 18$ cm.

questions.question_text: A solid metallic right circular cylinder of base diameter 16 cm and height 2 cm is melted and recast into a right circular cone of height three times that of the cylinder. Find the curved surface area of the cone. [Use π = 3.14]
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the cone be $r$ cm.
Then, $\pi \times 8^2 \times 2 = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times r^2 \times 6 \Rightarrow r = 8$.
Slant height, $l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{8^2 + 6^2} = \sqrt{100} \text{cm} = 10$ cm.
Curved surface area of cone = $\pi rl = (3.14 \times 8 \times 10) \text{cm}^2 = 251.2 \text{cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: A right cylindrical vessel is full of water. How many right cones having the same radius and height as those of the right cylinder will be needed to store that water?
questions.solution:
Let radius of each be $r$ and height of each be $h$.
Then, number of cones needed
$= \frac{\text{Volume of cylinder}}{\text{Volume of 1 cone}} = \frac{\pi r^2 h}{\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h} = 3$.

questions.question_text: A solid metallic cylinder of base radius 3 cm and height 5 cm is melted to form cones, each of height 1 cm and base radius 1 mm. The number of cones is
questions.solution:
Volume of cylinder = $(\pi \times 3 \times 3 \times 5) \text{cm}^3 = 45\pi \text{cm}^3$.
Volume of 1 cone = $\left(\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{10} \times \frac{1}{10} \times 1\right) \text{cm}^3 = \frac{\pi}{300} \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore$ Number of cones = $\left(45\pi \times \frac{300}{\pi}\right) = 13500$.

questions.question_text: Ice cream completely filled in a cylinder of diameter 35 cm and height 32 cm is to be served by completely filling identical disposable cones of diameter 4 cm and height 7 cm. The maximum number of persons that can be served this way is
questions.solution:
Volume of cylinder = $\left(\pi \times \frac{35}{2} \times \frac{35}{2} \times 32\right) \text{cm}^3 = 9800\pi \text{cm}^3$.
Volume of 1 cone = $\left(\frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 2 \times 2 \times 7\right) \text{cm}^3 = \frac{28\pi}{3} \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore$ Number of persons that can be served
$= \left(9800\pi \times \frac{3}{28\pi}\right) = 1050$.

questions.question_text: A solid cylinder and a solid cone have equal base and equal height. If the radius and height be in the ratio of 4 : 3, the ratio of the total surface area of the cylinder to that of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the cone and the cylinder be $4x$ and $3x$ respectively.
Then, total surface area of cylinder
$= [2\pi (4x) (4x + 3x)] \text{sq. units} = (8\pi x \cdot 7x) \text{sq. units} = (56\pi x^2) \text{sq. units}$.
Slant height of cone, $l = \sqrt{(4x)^2 + (3x)^2} = \sqrt{25x^2} = 5x$.
Total surface area of cone = $\pi r(l + r) = \pi \cdot 4x (5x + 4x) = (36\pi x^2) \text{sq. units}$.
$\therefore$ Required ratio = $\frac{56\pi x^2}{36\pi x^2} = 14 : 9$.

questions.question_text: Water flows at the rate of 10 metres per minute from a cylindrical pipe 5 mm in diameter. How long will it take to fill up a conical vessel whose diameter at the base is 40 cm and depth 24 cm?
questions.solution:
Volume flown in conical vessel = $\frac{1}{3} \pi (20)^2 \times 24 = 3200\pi$.
Volume flown in 1 min. = $\left(\pi \times \frac{2.5}{10} \times \frac{2.5}{10} \times 1000\right) = 62.5\pi$.
$\therefore$ Time taken = $\left(\frac{3200\pi}{62.5\pi}\right) = 51 \text{min. } 12 \text{sec}$.

questions.question_text: A conical flask has base radius a cm and height h cm. it is completely filled with milk. The milk is poured into a cylindrical thermos flask whose base radius is p cm. What will be the height of the milk level in the flask?
questions.solution:
Volume of milk in conical flask = $\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi a^2 h\right) \text{cm}^3$.
Let the height of the milk in the cylindrical flask be $x$ cm.
Then, volume of milk in cylindrical flask = $(\pi p^2 x) \text{cm}^3$.
$\therefore \frac{1}{3} \pi a^2 h = \pi p^2 x \Rightarrow x = \frac{a^2 h}{3p^2} \text{cm}$.

questions.question_text: A solid cylindrical block of radius 12 cm and height 18 cm is mounted with a conical block of radius 12 cm and height 5 cm. The total lateral surface of the solid thus formed is
questions.solution:
Slant height of the cone, $l = \sqrt{(12)^2 + (5)^2} = 13$ cm.
Lateral surface of the solid = Curved surface of cone + Curved surface of cylinder + Surface area of bottom
$= \pi rl + 2\pi rh + \pi r^2$, where $h$ is the height of the cylinder
$= \pi r (l + h + r) = \pi \times 12 \times (13 + 18 + 12) \text{cm}^2$
$= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 12 \times 43\right) \text{cm}^2 = \frac{11352}{7} \text{cm}^2 = 1621 \frac{5}{7} \text{cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: A tent is in the form of a right circular cylinder surmounted by a cone. The diameter of the cylinder is 24 m. The height of the cylindrical portion is 11 m while the vertex of the cone is 16 m above the ground. The area of the canvas required for the tent is
questions.solution:
Radius, $r = 12$ m.
Height of conical part, $h = (16 - 11) \text{m} = 5$ m.
Slant height of conical part,
$l = \sqrt{r^2 + h^2} = \sqrt{(12)^2 + 5^2} = \sqrt{169} = 13$ m.
Height of cylindrical part, $H = 11$ m.
Area of canvas required = Curved surface area of cylinder + Curved surface area of cone
$= 2\pi rH + \pi rl$
$= \frac{22}{7} (2 \times 12 \times 11 + 12 \times 13) \text{m}^2$
$= \frac{22}{7} (264 + 156) \text{m}^2 = \frac{22}{7} \times 420 \text{m}^2 = 1320 \text{m}^2$.

questions.question_text: A fountain having the shape of a right circular cone is fitted into a cylindrical tank of volume V so that the base of the tank coincides with the base of the cone and the height of the tank is the same as that of the cone. The volume of water in the tank, when it is completely filled with water from the fountain, is
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the tank be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, $V = \pi r^2 h$.
$\therefore$ Volume of water in the tank = Vol. of cylinder - Vol. of cone
$= \pi r^2 h - \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h = \frac{2}{3} \pi r^2 h = \frac{2}{3} V$.

questions.question_text: In a right circular cone, the radius of its base is 7 cm and its height is 24 cm. A cross-section is made through the mid-point of the height parallel to the base. The volume of the upper portion is
questions.solution:
$r=7$ cm, $h=24$ cm.
Now, $\Delta AOB \sim \Delta COD$.
So, $\frac{OA}{OC} = \frac{AB}{CD} = \frac{h/2}{CD} \Rightarrow CD = \frac{r}{2}$.
$\therefore$ Volume of upper portion
$= \frac{1}{3} \pi \left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^2 \left(\frac{h}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{7}{2} \times \frac{7}{2} \times 12$ cm$^3$
$= 154$ cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: A right circular cone is divided into two portions by a plane parallel to the base and passing through a point which is $ rac{1}{3}$rd of the height from the top. The ratio of the volume of the smaller cone to that of the remaining frustum of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let the radius and height of the cone be $r$ and $h$ respectively.
Then, $AB=r, OA=h, OC=\frac{h}{3}$.
Now, $\Delta AOB \sim \Delta COD$.
$\therefore \frac{AB}{CD} = \frac{OA}{OC} = \frac{h}{h/3} \Rightarrow CD = \frac{r}{3}$.
Volume of bigger cone $= \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h$.
Volume of smaller cone $= \frac{1}{3} \pi \left(\frac{r}{3}\right)^2 \left(\frac{h}{3}\right) = \frac{1}{27} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right)$.
Volume of frustum $= \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h - \frac{1}{27} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right) = \frac{26}{27} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right)$.
Hence, required ratio $= \frac{1}{27} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right) : \frac{26}{27} \left(\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h\right) = 1 : 26$.

questions.question_text: A bucket is in the form of a frustum of a cone and holds 28.490 litres of water. The radii of the top and bottom are 28 cm and 21 cm respectively. Find the height of the bucket.
questions.solution:
Volume of bucket $= 28.490$ litres $= (28.490 \times 1000)$ cm$^3$
$= 28490$ cm$^3$.
Let the height of the bucket be $h$ cm.
We have : $r = 21$ cm, $R = 28$ cm.
$\therefore \frac{1}{3} \pi h [R^2 + r^2 + Rr] = 28490$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times h [28^2 + 21^2 + 28 \times 21] = 28490$
$\Rightarrow h (784 + 441 + 588) = \frac{28490 \times 21}{22}$
$\Rightarrow 1813 h = 27195 \Rightarrow h = \frac{27195}{1813} = 15$ cm.

questions.question_text: A cone of height 10 cm and radius 5 cm is cut into two parts at half its height. The cut is given parallel to its circular base. What is the ratio of the curved surface area of the original cone and the curved surface area of the frustum?
questions.solution:
We have, $\Delta AOB \sim \Delta COD$.
$\therefore \frac{AB}{CD} = \frac{OA}{OC} = \frac{10}{5} \Rightarrow CD = \frac{5}{2}$ cm.
Curved surface area of the cone
$= [\pi \times 5 \times \sqrt{5^2 + 10^2}]$ cm$^2$
$= 25\sqrt{5} \pi$ cm$^2$.
Curved surface area of the frustum
$= \pi (R + r) l = \pi \left(5 + \frac{5}{2}\right) \sqrt{\left(5 - \frac{5}{2}\right)^2 + 5^2}$
$= \pi \times \frac{15}{2} \times \sqrt{\frac{25}{4} + 25} = \pi \times \frac{15}{2} \times \frac{5\sqrt{5}}{2} = \frac{75\sqrt{5}}{4} \pi$ cm$^2$.
Hence, required ratio $= 25\sqrt{5} \pi : \frac{75\sqrt{5}}{4} \pi = 4 : 3$.

questions.question_text: A sphere, cylinder and cone of dimensions radius = r cm and height = 2r cm are made. Which one has the greatest volume?
questions.solution:
Volume of sphere $= \left(\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3\right)$ cm$^3$.
Volume of cylinder $= [\pi r^2 \cdot (2r)]$ cm$^3 = (2 \pi r^3)$ cm$^3$.
Volume of cone $= \left[\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 \cdot (2r)\right]$ cm$^3 = \left(\frac{2}{3} \pi r^3\right)$ cm$^3$.
Clearly, cylinder has the greatest volume.

questions.passage_text:
Consider the volumes of the following
1. A parallelopiped of length 5 cm, breadth 3 cm and height 4 cm
2. A cube of each side 4 cm
3. A cylinder of radius 3 cm and length 3 cm
4. A sphere of radius 3 cm
questions.question_text: The volumes of these in the decreasing order is :
questions.solution:
Volume of parallelopiped $= (5 \times 3 \times 4)$ cm$^3 = 60$ cm$^3$.
Volume of cube $= (4)^3$ cm$^3 = 64$ cm$^3$.
Volume of cylinder $= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times 3 \times 3\right)$ cm$^3 = 84.86$ cm$^3$.
Volume of sphere $= \left(\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 3 \times 3 \times 3\right)$ cm$^3 = 113.14$ cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: The volume of a sphere is $2145 rac{11}{21} cm^3$. Its radius is equal to
questions.solution:
$\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^3 = 4851 \Rightarrow r^3 = \left(\frac{4851 \times 3 \times 7}{4 \times 22}\right) = 512$
$\Rightarrow r = \sqrt[3]{512} = 8$ cm.

questions.question_text: The volume of a sphere is 4851 cu. cm. Its curved surface area is
questions.solution:
$\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^3 = 4851 \Rightarrow r^3 = \left(4851 \times \frac{3}{4} \times \frac{7}{22}\right) = \left(\frac{21}{2}\right)^3$
$\Rightarrow r = \frac{21}{2}$.
$\therefore$ Curved surface area
$= \left(4 \times \frac{22}{7} \times \frac{21}{2} \times \frac{21}{2}\right)$ cm$^2 = 1386$ cm$^2$.

questions.question_text: The curved surface area of a sphere is 5544 sq. cm. Its volume is
questions.solution:
$4\pi r^2 = 5544 \Rightarrow r^2 = \left(5544 \times \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{7}{22}\right) = 441 \Rightarrow r = 21$.
$\therefore$ Volume $= \left(\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21 \times 21\right)$ cm$^3 = 38808$ cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: The volume of a sphere of radius r is obtained by multiplying its surface area by
questions.solution: Volume $= \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 = (4\pi r^2) \times \frac{r}{3} = \frac{r}{3} \times \text{Surface area}$.

questions.question_text: For a sphere of radius 10 cm, what percent of the numerical value of its volume would be the numerical value of the surface area?
questions.solution:
Volume of the sphere $= \left[\frac{4}{3} \pi (10)^3\right]$ cm$^3$. Surface area of
the sphere $= [4\pi (10)^2]$ cm$^2$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage $= \left[\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi (10)^3}{4 \pi (10)^2} \times 100\right] \% = 30 \%$.

questions.question_text: If the volume of a sphere is divided by its surface area, the result is 27 cm. The radius of the sphere is
questions.solution: $\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3}{\pi r^2} = 27 \Rightarrow r = 81$ cm.

questions.question_text: If the radii of two spheres are in the ratio $1 : 4$, then their surface areas are in the ratio
questions.solution:
Let the radii of the two spheres be $r$ and $4r$ respectively.
Then, required ratio $= \frac{4\pi r^2}{4\pi (4r)^2} = \frac{r^2}{16r^2} = 1:16$.

questions.question_text: The radii of two spheres are in the ratio $3 : 2$. Their volumes will be in the ratio
questions.solution:
Let the radii of the two spheres be $3r$ and $2r$ respectively.
Then, required ratio $= \frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi (3r)^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi (2r)^3} = \frac{27}{8} = 27:8$.

questions.question_text: Surface area of a sphere is 2464 cm$^2$. If its radius be doubled, then the surface area of the new sphere will be :
questions.solution:
Let the original radius be $r$. Then, original surface area
$= 4\pi r^2 = 2464$ cm$^2$ (given).
New radius $= 2r$. $\therefore$ New surface area $= 4\pi (2r)^2$
$= 4 \times 4\pi r^2 = (4 \times 2464)$ cm$^2 = 9856$ cm$^2$.

questions.question_text: If the radius of a sphere is doubled, how many times does its volume become?
questions.solution:
Let the original radius be $r$. Then, original volume $= \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3$.
New radius $= 2r$.
$\therefore$ New volume $= \frac{4}{3} \pi (2r)^3 = 8 \times \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 = 8 \times \text{original volume}$.

questions.question_text: If the radius of a sphere is increased by 2 cm, then its surface area increases by 352 cm$^2$. The radius of the sphere before the increase was
questions.solution:
$4\pi (r + 2)^2 - 4\pi r^2 = 352 \Leftrightarrow (r + 2)^2 - r^2 = \left(352 \times \frac{7}{22} \times \frac{1}{4}\right) = 28$.
$\Leftrightarrow (r + 2 + r) (r + 2 - r) = 28 \Leftrightarrow 2r + 2 = 14$
$\Rightarrow r = \left(\frac{14}{2} - 1\right) = 6$ cm.

questions.question_text: If the measured value of the radius is 1.5% larger, the percentage error (correct to one decimal place) made in calculating the volume of a sphere is
questions.solution:
Let the correct radius be 100 cm.
Then, measured radius $= 101.5$ cm.
$\therefore$ Error in volume $= \frac{4}{3} \pi [(101.5)^3 - (100)^3]$ cm$^3$
$= \frac{4}{3} \pi (1045678.375 - 1000000)$ cm$^3$
$= \left(\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 45678.375\right)$ cm$^3$.
$\therefore$ Error $\% = \left[\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi (45678.375)}{\frac{4}{3} \pi (100 \times 100 \times 100)} \times 100\right] \% = 4.56\%$
$= 4.6\%$ (app.).

questions.question_text: The volumes of two spheres are in the ratio of $64 : 27$. The ratio of their surface areas is
questions.solution:
Let their radii be $R$ and $r$. Then,
$\frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3} = \frac{64}{27} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^3 = \frac{64}{27} = \left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^3 \Rightarrow \frac{R}{r} = \frac{4}{3}$.
Ratio of surface areas $= \frac{4\pi R^2}{4\pi r^2} = \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{16}{9}$.

questions.question_text: If the surface areas of two spheres are in the ratio of $4 : 25$, then the ratio of their volumes is
questions.solution:
Let their radii be $R$ and $r$.
Then, $\frac{4\pi R^2}{4\pi r^2} = \frac{4}{25} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{2}{5}\right)^2 \Rightarrow \frac{R}{r} = \frac{2}{5}$.
$\therefore$ Ratio of volumes $= \frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3}{\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3} = \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^3 = \left(\frac{2}{5}\right)^3 = \frac{8}{125}$.

questions.question_text: If the volume and surface area of a sphere are numerically the same, then its radius is
If three metallic spheres of radii 6 cms, 8 cms and 10 cms are melted to form a single sphere, the diameter of the new sphere will be
A solid metallic sphere of radius 8 cm is melted and recast into spherical balls each of radius 2 cm. The number of spherical balls, thus obtained, is
A spherical ball of lead, 3 cm in diameter is melted and recast into three spherical balls. The diameter of two of these are 1.5 cm and 2 cm respectively. The diameter of the third ball is
If a solid sphere of radius 10 cm is moulded into 8 spherical solid balls of equal radius, then the surface area of each ball is
A hollow spherical metallic ball has an external diameter 6 cm and is $\frac{1}{2}$ cm thick. The volume of metal used in the ball is
A solid piece of iron of dimensions $49 \times 33 \times 24$ cm is moulded into a sphere. The radius of the sphere is
How many bullets can be made out of a cube of lead whose edge measures 22 cm, each bullet being 2 cm in diameter?
The volume of the largest sphere which can be carved out of a cube of side 6 cm is
How many lead shots each 3 mm in diameter can be made from a cuboid of dimensions 9 cm $\times$ 11 cm $\times$ 12 cm?
A sphere and a cube have equal surface areas. The ratio of the volume of the sphere to that of the cube is
The ratio of the volume of a cube to that of a sphere which will fit inside the cube is
The volume of the largest possible cube that can be inscribed in a hollow spherical ball of radius $r$ cm is
A right circular cylinder and a sphere are of equal volumes and their radii are also equal. If $h$ is the height of the cylinder and $d$, the diameter of the sphere, then
The surface area of a sphere is same as the curved surface area of a right circular cylinder whose height and diameter are 12 cm each. The radius of the sphere is
The diameter of the iron ball used for the shot-put game is 14 cm. It is melted and then a solid cylinder of height $1\frac{2}{3}$ cm is made. What will be the diameter of the base of the cylinder?
A solid metallic sphere of radius $r$ is converted into a solid right circular cylinder of radius $R$. If the height of the cylinder is twice the radius of the sphere, then

The ratio of the volumes of a right circular cylinder and a sphere is $3 : 2$. If the radius of the sphere is double the radius of the base of the cylinder, find the ratio of the total surface areas of the cylinder and the sphere.
The volume of the greatest sphere that can be cut off from a cylindrical log of wood of base radius 1 cm and height 5 cm is
How many spherical bullets can be made out of a lead cylinder 15 cm high and with base radius 3 cm, each bullet being 5 mm in diameter?
A cylindrical rod of iron whose height is eight times its radius is melted and cast into spherical balls each of half the radius of the cylinder. The number of spherical balls is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the cylindrical rod be $r$.
Then, height of the rod = $8r$ and radius of one ball = $ rac{r}{2}$.
Number of balls = $ rac{\pi r^2 \times 8r}{\frac{4}{3} \pi \left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^3} = \frac{8r^3}{\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{r^3}{8}} = \frac{8 \times 3 \times 8}{4} = 48$.

questions.question_text: A copper wire of length 36 m and diameter 2 mm is melted to form a sphere. The radius of the sphere (in cm) is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the sphere be $r$ cm. Then,
$\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 = \pi \times (0.1)^2 \times 3600 \Rightarrow r^3 = 36 \times 27 = 27 \Rightarrow r = 3$ cm.

questions.question_text: The diameter of a sphere is 8 cm. It is melted and drawn into a wire of diameter 3 mm. The length of the wire is
questions.solution:
Let the length of the wire be $h$. Then,
$\pi \times \frac{1}{20} \times \frac{1}{20} \times h = \pi \times 4 \times 4 \times 4$
$\Leftrightarrow h = \frac{4 \times 4 \times 4 \times 20 \times 20}{3 \times 3} \text{ cm} = \left(\frac{102400}{27}\right) \text{ cm}$
$= 3792.5 \text{ cm} = 37.9 \text{ m}$.

questions.question_text: A cylindrical vessel of radius 4 cm contains water. A solid sphere of radius 3 cm is lowered into the water until it is completely immersed. The water level in the vessel will rise by :
questions.solution:
Let the rise in the water level be $h$ cm.
Then, $\pi \times 4 \times 4 \times h = \pi \times 3 \times 3 \times 3 \Rightarrow h = \left(\frac{3 \times 3 \times 3}{4 \times 4}\right) = \frac{9}{4} \text{ cm}$.

questions.question_text: The ratio of the surface area of a sphere and the curved surface area of the cylinder circumscribing the sphere is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the sphere be $r$.
Then, radius of the cylinder = $r$.
height of the cylinder = $2r$.
Surface area of sphere = $4\pi r^2$.
Surface area of the cylinder = $2\pi r (2r) = 4\pi r^2$.
Required ratio = $4\pi r^2 : 4\pi r^2 = 1 : 1$.

questions.question_text: 12 spheres of the same size are made from melting a solid cylinder of 16 cm diameter and 2 cm height. The diameter of each sphere is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of each sphere be $r$ cm.
Then, Volume of 12 spheres = Volume of cylinder
$\Rightarrow 12 \times \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 = \pi \times 8 \times 8 \times 2 \Rightarrow r^3 = \left(\frac{8 \times 8 \times 2 \times 3}{12 \times 4}\right)$
$\Rightarrow r = 2$ cm.
Diameter of each sphere = $2r = 4$ cm.

questions.question_text: A spherical iron ball is dropped into a cylindrical vessel of base diameter 14 cm containing water. The water level is increased by $1\frac{1}{3}$ cm. What is radius of the ball?
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the ball be $r$ cm.
Volume of ball = Volume of water displaced by it
$\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3 = \pi \times 7 \times 7 \times \frac{28}{3} \Rightarrow r^3 = 7^3 \Rightarrow r = 7$ cm.

questions.question_text: If a hollow sphere of internal and external diameters 4 cm and 8 cm respectively is melted into a cylinder of base diameter 8 cm, then the height of the cylinder is
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cylinder be $h$ cm.
Then, $\frac{4}{3} \pi (4^3 - 2^3) = \pi \times 4^2 \times h$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \times 56 = 16h \Rightarrow h = \frac{56}{16 \times 3} = \frac{14}{3}$ cm.

questions.question_text: Each of the measures of the radius of the base of a cone and that of a sphere is 8 cm. Also, the volumes of these two solids are equal. The slant height of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cone be $h$ cm.
Then, $\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 8 \times 8 \times 14 = \frac{1}{3} \pi \times 4^2 \times h \Rightarrow h = \frac{8 \times 8 \times 14}{16} = 32$ cm.

questions.question_text: A metallic sphere of radius 5 cm is melted to make a cone with base of the same radius. What is the height of the cone?
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cone be $h$ cm.
Then, $\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 5^3 = \frac{1}{3} \pi \times 5^2 \times h \Rightarrow h = 20$ cm.

questions.question_text: Some solid metallic right circular cones, each with radius of the base 3 cm and height 4 cm, are melted to form a solid sphere of radius 6 cm. The number of right circular cones is
questions.solution:
Volume of sphere = $\left(\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 6^3\right) \text{ cm}^3 = (288 \pi) \text{ cm}^3$.
Volume of each cone = $\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 3^2 \times 4\right) \text{ cm}^3 = (12 \pi) \text{ cm}^3$.
Number of cones = $\frac{288 \pi}{12 \pi} = 24$.

questions.question_text: A metallic sphere of radius 10.5 cm is melted and recast into small right circular cones, each of base radius 3.5 cm and height 3 cm. The number of cones so formed is
questions.solution:
Volume of sphere = $\left[\frac{4}{3} \pi \times (10.5)^3\right] \text{ cm}^3 = (4 \pi \times 10.5 \times 10.5 \times 3.5) \text{ cm}^3$.
Volume of each cone = $\left[\frac{1}{3} \pi \times (3.5)^2 \times 3\right] \text{ cm}^3 = (\pi \times 3.5 \times 3.5) \text{ cm}^3$.
Number of cones formed = $\frac{4 \times 10.5 \times 10.5 \times 3.5}{3.5 \times 3.5} = 126$.

questions.question_text: A cone of height 15 cm and base diameter 30 cm is carved out of a wooden sphere of radius 15 cm. The percentage of wood wasted is
questions.solution:
Volume of sphere = $\left(\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15\right) \text{ cm}^3$.
Volume of cone = $\left(\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15\right) \text{ cm}^3$.
Volume of wood wasted = $\left[\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15 - \frac{1}{3} \pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15\right] \text{ cm}^3 = (\pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15) \text{ cm}^3$.
Required percentage = $\left(\frac{\pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15}{\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 15 \times 15 \times 15} \times 100\right) \% = 75\%$.

questions.question_text: A metallic cone of radius 12 cm and height 24 cm is melted and made into spheres of radius 2 cm each. How many spheres are there?
questions.solution: Number of spheres = $\frac{\text{Volume of cone}}{\text{Volume of 1 sphere}} = \frac{\frac{1}{3} \pi \times 12 \times 12 \times 24}{\frac{4}{3} \pi \times 2 \times 2 \times 2} = 108$.

questions.question_text: In what ratio are the volumes of a cylinder, a cone and a sphere, if each has the same diameter and the same height?
questions.solution:
Let radius = $R$ and height = $H$.
Then, Ratio of their volumes = $\frac{1}{3} \pi R^2 H : \frac{1}{3} \pi R^3 : \frac{2}{3} \pi R^3 = H : R : 2R = 3 : 1 : 2$. (In sphere, $H = 2R$ or $R = \frac{H}{2}$)

questions.question_text: If the diameter of a sphere is 6 m, its hemisphere will have a volume of
questions.solution: Volume of hemisphere = $\left(\frac{2}{3} \pi \times 3 \times 3 \times 3\right) \text{ m}^3 = (18 \pi) \text{ m}^3$.

questions.question_text: The total surface area of a solid hemisphere of diameter 14 cm, is
questions.solution: Total surface area = $3 \pi R^2 = \left(3 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7\right) \text{ cm}^2 = 462 \text{ cm}^2$.

questions.question_text: Volume of a hemisphere is 19404 cu. cm. Its radius is
questions.solution:
Let the radius be $R$ cm.
Then, $\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times R^3 = 19404 \Leftrightarrow R^3 = \left(19404 \times \frac{21}{44}\right) = (21)^3 \Leftrightarrow R = 21$ cm.

questions.question_text: A hemispherical bowl is 176 cm round the brim. Supposing it to be half full, how many persons may be served from it in hemispherical glasses 4 cm in diameter at the top?
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the hemispherical bowl be $r$ cm.
Then, $2 \pi r = 176 \Rightarrow r = \frac{176 \times 7}{2 \times 22} = 28$.
Volume of liquid in the bowl = $\left(\frac{2}{3} \pi \times 28 \times 28 \times 28\right) \text{ cm}^3 = \left(\frac{2}{3} \pi \times 14 \times 28 \times 28\right) \text{ cm}^3$.
Volume of 1 glass = $\left(\frac{2}{3} \pi \times 2 \times 2 \times 2\right) \text{ cm}^3$.
Required number of persons = $\frac{\text{Volume of liquid in the bowl}}{\text{Volume of 1 glass}} = \frac{14 \times 28 \times 28}{2 \times 2 \times 2} = 1372$.

questions.question_text: The capacities of two hemispherical vessels are 6.4 litres and 21.6 litres. The areas of inner curved surfaces of the vessels will be in the ratio of
questions.solution:
Let their radii be $R$ and $r$. Then,
$\frac{2}{3} \pi R^3 / \frac{2}{3} \pi r^3 = \frac{6.4}{21.6} \Leftrightarrow \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^3 = \frac{8}{27} = \left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^3 \Leftrightarrow \frac{R}{r} = \frac{2}{3}$.
Ratio of curved surface areas = $\frac{2 \pi R^2}{2 \pi r^2} = \left(\frac{R}{r}\right)^2 = \frac{4}{9}$.

questions.question_text: A hemispherical bowl is made of steel 0.5 cm thick. The inside radius of the bowl is 4 cm. The volume of steel used in making the bowl is
questions.solution:
Internal radius = 4 cm; External radius = 4.5 cm.
Volume of steel used in making the bowl.
= $\left[\frac{2}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times\{(4.5)^3 - 4^3\}\right]$ cm$^3$
= $\left(\frac{2}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times 27.125\right)$ cm$^3$
= $\left(\frac{2\times 22\times 3.875}{3}\right)$ cm$^3$ = $\left(\frac{170.5}{3}\right)$ cm$^3$ = 56.83 cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: The external and internal diameters of a hemispherical bowl are 10 cm and 8 cm respectively. What is the total surface area of the bowl?
questions.solution:
Internal radius, r = 4 cm; External radius, R = 5 cm.
Total surface area = $2\pi R^2 + 2\pi r^2 + \pi (R^2 - r^2)$
= $3\pi R^2 + \pi r^2 = [\pi(3 \times 25 + 16)]$ cm$^2$.
= $\left(\frac{22}{7}\times 91\right)$ cm$^2$ = 286 cm$^2$.

questions.question_text: A hemispherical bowl of internal radius 12 cm contains liquid. This liquid is to be filled into cylindrical container of diameter 4 cm and height 3 cm. The number of containers that is necessary to empty the bowl is
questions.solution:
Volume of hemispherical bowl = $\left(\frac{2}{3}\times\pi\times 12\times 12\times 12\right)$ cm$^3$.
Volume of 1 cylindrical container = $(\pi \times 2 \times 2 \times 3)$ cm$^3$.
Number of containers required = $\frac{\frac{2}{3}\times 12\times 12\times 12}{2\times 2\times 3} = 96$.

questions.question_text: A hemispherical bowl is filled to the brim with a beverage. The contents of the bowl are transferred into a cylindrical vessel whose radius is 50% more than its height. If the diameter is same for both the bowl and the cylinder, the volume of the beverage in the cylindrical vessel is
questions.option_d: More than 100% (i.e., some liquid will be left in the bowl)
questions.solution:
Let the height of the vessel be x. Then, radius of the bowl
= radius of the vessel = $\frac{x}{2}$.
Volume of the bowl, $V_1 = \frac{2}{3}\pi\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^3 = \frac{1}{12}\pi x^3$.
Volume of the vessel, $V_2 = \pi\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^2 x = \frac{1}{4}\pi x^3$.
Since $V_2 > V_1$, so the vessel can contain 100% of the beverage filled in the bowl.

questions.question_text: A water tank open at the top is hemispherical at the bottom and cylindrical above it. If radius of the hemisphere is 12 m and the total capacity of the tank is $3312\pi$ m$^3$, then the ratio of the surface areas of the hemispherical and the cylindrical portions is
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cylindrical part be h metres.
Volume of the tank = Volume of hemispherical part + Volume of cylindrical part
= $\left(\frac{2}{3}\pi\times 12\times 12\times 12 + \pi\times 12\times 12\times h\right)$ m$^3$
= $\pi (1152 + 144 h)$ m$^3$
$\pi (1152 + 144 h) = 3312 \pi \Rightarrow 144 h = 2160 \Rightarrow h = 15$ m.
So, ratio of surface areas = $\frac{2\pi r^2}{2\pi rh} = \frac{12}{15} = 4:5$.

questions.question_text: If a cylindrical tower D metres in diameter and H metres high is capped with a semi-spherical dome, then the total visible surface of the tower will be
questions.solution:
Total visible surface area
= Curved surface area of cylinder + Curved surface area of hemisphere =
$\left[2\pi\frac{D}{2}\times H + 2\pi\left(\frac{D}{2}\right)^2\right]$ m$^2 = \left(\pi DH + \frac{\pi D^2}{2}\right)$ m$^2$
= $\left[\frac{\pi D}{2}(2H + D)\right]$ m$^2$.

questions.question_text: The ratio of the volumes of a hemisphere and a cylinder circumscribing this hemisphere and having a common base is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the hemisphere be r cm.
Then, radius of the cylinder = r cm.
height of the cylinder = r cm.
Required ratio =
$\frac{\text{Volume of hemisphere}}{\text{Volume of cylinder}} = \frac{\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3}{\pi r^2 \times r} = \frac{2}{3}$.

questions.question_text: A metallic hemisphere is melted and recast in the shape of a cone with the same base radius (R) as that of the hemisphere. If H is the height of the cone, then
questions.solution: $\frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H = \frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H \Rightarrow H = 2R$.

questions.question_text: A hemisphere of lead of radius 6 cm is cast into a right circular cone of height 75 cm. The radius of the base of the cone is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the cone be R cm.
Then, $\frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 \times 75 = \frac{1}{3}\pi \times 6\times 6\times 6$
$\Leftrightarrow R^2 = \left(\frac{2\times 6\times 6\times 6}{75}\right) = \left(\frac{144}{25}\right) \Rightarrow R = \frac{12}{5}$ cm = 2.4 cm.

questions.question_text: A hemisphere and a cone have equal bases. If their heights are also equal, then the ratio of their curved surfaces will be
questions.solution:
Let the radius of each be R. Height of hemisphere, H = R.
So, height of cone = height of hemisphere = R.
Slant height of cone = $\sqrt{R^2 + R^2} = R\sqrt{2}$.
$\frac{\text{Curved surface area of hemisphere}}{\text{Curved surface area of cone}} = \frac{2\pi R^2}{\pi R \times R\sqrt{2}} = \sqrt{2}:1$.

questions.question_text: If the radius of the base and height of a cylinder and cone are each equal to r, and the radius of a hemisphere is also equal to r, then the volumes of the cone, cylinder and hemisphere are in the ratio
questions.solution:
Required ratio = Volume of cone : Volume of cylinder : Volume of hemisphere
= $\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h : \pi r^2 h : \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3 = 1 : 3 : 2$.

questions.question_text: A solid body is made up of a cylinder of radius r and height r, a cone of base radius r and height r fixed to the cylinder’s one base and a hemisphere of radius r to its other base. The total volume of the body (given r = 2) is
questions.solution:
Total volume of the body
= Volume of the cylinder + Volume of the cone + Volume of the hemisphere
= $\pi r^2 h + \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h + \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3 = 2\pi r^3 = 2\pi. (2)^3 = 16\pi$.

questions.question_text: A solid cylinder of base radius 7 cm and height 24 cm is surmounted by a cone of the same radius and same vertical height. A hemisphere surmounts the cylinder at the other end. Surface area of the solid will be
questions.solution:
Surface area of the solid =
Curved surface area of cone
+ Curved surface area of cylinder
+ Curved surface area of hemisphere
= $\left(\pi\times 7\times\sqrt{7^2 + (24)^2} + 2\pi\times 7\times 24 + 2\pi\times 7\times 7\right)$ cm$^2$
= (175$\pi$ + 336$\pi$ + 98$\pi$) cm$^2$ = (609$\pi$) cm$^2$.

questions.question_text: A solid is in the form of a right circular cylinder with hemispherical ends. The total length of the solid is 35 cm. The diameter of the cylinder is $\frac{1}{4}$ of its height. The surface area of the solid is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the cylinder and the hemisphere be r cm.
Diameter of the cylinder = (2r) cm.
Height of the cylinder = (4 $\times$ 2r) cm = (8r) cm.
Total length of the solid = (8r + r + r) cm = (10r) cm.
10r = 35 $\Rightarrow$ r = 3.5 cm.
Surface area of the solid
= Curved surface area of the cylinder
+ 2 $\times$ (curved surface area of the hemisphere)
= $\left(2\times\frac{22}{7}\times 3.5\times 28 + 2\times 2\times\frac{22}{7}\times 3.5\times 3.5\right)$ cm$^2$
= (616 + 154) cm$^2$ = 770 cm$^2$.

questions.question_text: A sphere of maximum volume is cut out from a solid hemisphere of radius r. The ratio of the volume of the hemisphere to that of the cut out sphere is :
questions.solution:
Volume of hemisphere = $\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$.
Volume of biggest sphere = Volume of sphere with diameter r = $\frac{4}{3}\pi\left(\frac{r}{2}\right)^3 = \frac{1}{6}\pi r^3$.
Required ratio = $\frac{\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3}{\frac{1}{6}\pi r^3} = 4:1$.

questions.question_text: What is the volume in cubic cm of a pyramid whose area of the base is 25 sq cm and height 9 cm?
questions.solution:
Volume of pyramid =
$\frac{1}{3}\times\text{area of base}\times\text{height} = \left(\frac{1}{3}\times 25\times 9\right)$ cm$^3$ = 75 cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: If a regular square pyramid has a base of side 8 cm and height 30 cm, its volume is
questions.solution: Volume of pyramid = $\left(\frac{1}{3}\times 8\times 30\right)$ cm$^3$ = 640 cm$^3$.

questions.question_text: The base of a pyramid is an equilateral triangle of side 1 m. If the height of the pyramid is 4 metres, then the volume is
questions.solution:
Area of the base = $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times 1^2\right)$ m$^2$ = $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}$ m$^2$.
Volume of pyramid = $\left(\frac{1}{3}\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times 4\right)$ m$^3$ = $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}\right)$ m$^3$
= $\left(\frac{1.732}{3}\right)$ m$^3$ = 0.577 m$^3$.

questions.question_text: A right pyramid is on a regular hexagonal base. Each side of the base is 10 m and the height is 60 m. The volume of the pyramid is
questions.solution:
Area of hexagonal base = $\left(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}\times(10)^2\right)$ m$^2$ = $150\sqrt{3}$ m$^2$.
Volume of pyramid = $\left(\frac{1}{3}\times 150\sqrt{3}\times 60\right)$ m$^3$ = $3000\sqrt{3}$ m$^3$
= (3000 $\times$ 1.732) m$^3$ = 5196 m$^3$.

questions.question_text: A pyramid has an equilateral triangle as its base of which each side is 1m. Its slant edge is 3 m. The whole surface area of the pyramid is equal to
questions.solution:
Area of base = $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times 1^2\right)$ m$^2$ = $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}$ m$^2$.
Clearly, the pyramid has 3 triangular faces each with sides 3 m, 3 m and 1 m.
So, area of each lateral face
= $\sqrt{\frac{7}{2}\times\left(\frac{7}{2}-3\right)\times\left(\frac{7}{2}-3\right)\times\left(\frac{7}{2}-1\right)}$ m$^2$ [s = $\frac{3+3+1}{2} = \frac{7}{2}$]
= $\sqrt{\frac{7}{2}\times\frac{1}{2}\times\frac{1}{2}\times\frac{5}{2}}$ m$^2$ = $\frac{\sqrt{35}}{4}$ m$^2$.
Whole surface area of the pyramid
= $\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} + 3\times\frac{\sqrt{35}}{4}\right)$ m$^2$ = $\frac{\sqrt{3}+3\sqrt{35}}{4}$ m$^2$.

questions.question_text: A right pyramid has an equilateral triangular base of side 4 units. If the number of square units of its whole surface area is three times the number of cubic units of its volume, find its height.
questions.solution:
Let the height of the pyramid be $h$ units.
Then, volume of the pyramid
$= \left[\frac{1}{3}\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times4\times4\right]h$ cu. units $= \left(\frac{4h}{\sqrt{3}}\right)$ cu. units.
Whole surface area of the pyramid
$= \left[4\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\times4\times4\right]$ sq. units $= (16\sqrt{3})$ sq. units.
$\therefore \frac{4h}{\sqrt{3}} = 16\sqrt{3} \Rightarrow h = 4$ units.

questions.question_text: Length of each edge, of a regular tetrahedron is 1 cm. Its volume is
questions.solution:
Length of each edge of a regular tetrahedron
$= 1$ cm
Volume of regular tetrahedron
$= \frac{a^3}{6\sqrt{2}}$ cm$^3$
$= \frac{1}{6\sqrt{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{6\sqrt{2}\times\sqrt{2}}$ cu.cm
$= \frac{\sqrt{2}}{12}$ cu.cm.

questions.question_text: The volume of a right circular cone which is obtained from a wooden cube of edge 4.2 dm wasting minimum amount of wood is
questions.solution:
The volume of cone should be maximum.
$\therefore$ Radius of the base of cone
$= \frac{\text{Edge of cube}}{2} = \frac{4.2}{2} = 2.1$ dm.
Height of cone $= \text{Edge of cube} = 4.2$ dm.
$\therefore$ Volume of cone $= \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$
$= \left(\frac{1}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times2.1\times2.1\times4.2\right)$ cu.dm.
$= 19.404$ cu.dm.

questions.question_text: Base of a right prism is a rectangle, the ratio of whose length and breadth is 3 : 2. If the height of the prism is 12 cm and total surface area is 288 sq. cm. the volume of the prism is
questions.solution:
Let the length of base be $3a$ cm and breadth be $2a$ cm
Total surface area of prism
$= [\text{perimeter of base} \times \text{height}] + [2 \times \text{area of base}]$
$= [2(3a + 2a) \times 12 + 2(3a \times 2a)]$ sq. cm.
$= (120a + 12a^2)$ sq. cm.
According to the question,
$120a + 12a^2 = 288$
$\Rightarrow a^2 + 10a = 24$
$\Rightarrow a^2 + 10a - 24 = 0$
$\Rightarrow a^2 + 12a - 2a - 24 = 0$
$\Rightarrow a(a + 12) - 2(a + 12) = 0$
$\Rightarrow (a - 2)(a + 12) = 0$
$\Rightarrow a = 2$ because $a \neq -12$
$\therefore$ Volume of prism $= \text{Area of base} \times \text{height}$
$= (3a \times 2a \times 12)$ cu. cm.
$= (72a^2) = (72 \times 2 \times 2)$ cu.cm.
$= 288$ cu.cm.

questions.question_text: The radius of a cylinder is 5m more than its height. If the curved surface area of the cylinder is 792m$^2$, what is the volume of the cylinder? (in m$^3$)
questions.solution:
Let the height of the cylinder be $x$ m.
Then, radius $= (x + 5)$m
Curved surface area of the cylinder $= 2\pi rh$
Now, $2\times\frac{22}{7}\times(x^2 + 5x) = 792$
$x^2 + 5x = \frac{792 \times 7}{44} = 126$
$\Rightarrow x^2 + 5x = 126$
$x^2 + 5x - 126 = 0$
$x^2 + 14x - 9x - 126 = 0$
$x(x + 14) - 9(x + 14) = 0$
$(x - 9)(x + 14) = 0$
$\therefore x = 9, -14$ (neglect negative value)
$\therefore$ Height of cylinder $= 9$m
$\therefore$ Radius of cylinder $= 9 + 5 = 14$m
Volume of cylinder $= \pi r^2 h$
$= \frac{22}{7}\times14\times14\times9 = 5544$m$^3$.

questions.question_text: The radius of base and curved surface area of a right cylinder is ‘r’ units and $4\pi rh$ square units respectively. The height of the cylinder is
questions.solution:
Radius of base $= r$ units
Curved surface area of a right cylinder $= 4\pi rh$
Curved surface area of cylinder $= 2\pi RH$
$\therefore$ According to the question, $2\pi RH = 4\pi rh$
$\Rightarrow$ Height of cylinder $= 2h$ units

questions.question_text: A hemispherical bowls has 3.5cm radius. It is to be painted inside as well as outside. The cost of painting it at the rate of ₹ 5 per 10sq. cm will be
questions.solution:
Radius of a hemispherical bowl $= 3.5$cm
Inner and outer surface areas of the bowl $= 4\pi r^2$
$= 4\times\frac{22}{7}\times3.5\times3.5$
$= 154$ sq. cm.
Total cost of painting at the rate of ₹ $5$ per $10$ sq. cm.
$= 154 \times \frac{5}{10} = ₹ 77$

questions.question_text: If the volume and curved surfaces area of a cylinder are 616 m$^3$ and 352m$^2$ respectively, what is the total surface area of the cylinder (in m$^2$)
questions.solution:
Volume of cylinder $= \pi r^2 h$
$\therefore$ Curved surface area of cylinder $= 2\pi rh$
$\therefore \frac{\pi r^2 h}{2\pi rh} = \frac{616}{352}$
$\Rightarrow r = \frac{2 \times 616}{352} = 3.5$ m
$\therefore$ volume of cylinder $= \pi r^2 h = 616$
$\Rightarrow \frac{22}{7}\times3.5\times3.5\times h = 616$
$\Rightarrow 11 \times 3.5 \times h = 616$
$\Rightarrow h = \frac{616}{11 \times 3.5} = 16$
$\therefore$ Total surface area of the cylinder
$= 2\pi rh + 2\pi r^2$
$= 2\pi r(h + r)$
$= 2\times\frac{22}{7}\times3.5(16 + 3.5)$
$= 2\times\frac{22}{7}\times3.5(19.5)$
$= 22 \times 19.5 = 429$ sq. m.

questions.question_text: The radius of a hemispherical bowls is 6cm. The capacity of the bowl is (Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
questions.solution:
Radius of hemisphere bowl $= 6$cm
$\therefore$ Volume of hemisphere $= \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3 = \frac{2}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times6\times6\times6$
$= \frac{9504}{21} = 452.57$ cm$^3$

questions.question_text: Each side of a cube is decreased by 25%. Find the ratio of the volumes of the original cube and the resulting cube.
questions.solution:
Let the side of cube $= 10$cm
$\therefore$ Original volume $= 10 \times 10 \times 10 = 1000$ cm$^3$
Now, side of new cube $= 10 - 25\%$ of $10 = 7.5$cm
$\therefore$ New volume $= 7.5 \times 7.5 \times 7.5 = 421.875$cm$^3$
$\therefore$ Required Ratio $= \frac{1000}{421.875} = \frac{1000000}{421875} = \frac{64}{27} = 64 : 27$

questions.question_text: A hemisphere and a cone have equal bases. If their heights are also equal, then the ratio of their curved surfaces will be
questions.solution:
Let
$OP = OQ = OR = r$
$\therefore OR = h = r$
$\therefore$ Curved surface area of the hemisphere $= 2\pi r^2$
Curved surface area of a cone $= \pi rl$
Where $l = \sqrt{h^2 + r^2} = \sqrt{r^2 + r^2} = r\sqrt{2}$
$\therefore$ Required ratio $= \frac{2\pi r^2}{\pi rl} = \frac{2r^2}{r \cdot r\sqrt{2}}$
$= \frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{2\sqrt{2}}{2} = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{1} = \sqrt{2} : 1$

questions.question_text: The base of a right prism is a trapezium whose lengths of two parallels sides are 10 cm and 6cm and distance between them is 5 cm. If the heights of the prism is 8cm, its volume is
questions.solution:
Length of parallel sides of prism $= 10$cm and $6$cm
Height of prism $= 8$cm
$\therefore$ Volume of prism $= \frac{1}{2}(10 + 6) \times 5 \times 8$
$= \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times 5 \times 8 = 320$ cm$^3$

questions.question_text: The sum of the radius and the height of a cylinder is 19 m. The total surface area of the cylinder is 1672m$^2$, what is the volume of the cylinder? (in m$^3$)
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the cylinder be $r$ and height be $h$.
Then, $r + h = 19$ ....(i)
Again, total surface area of cylinder $= (2\pi rh + 2\pi r^2)$
Now, $2\pi r(h + r) = 1672$
Or, $2\pi r \times 19 = 1672$
Or $38\pi r = 1672$
$\therefore \pi r = \frac{1672}{38} = 44$ m
$\therefore r = \frac{44 \times 7}{22} = 14$
$\therefore$ Height $= 19 - 14 = 5$m
Volume of cylinder $= \pi r^2 h$
$= \frac{22}{7}\times14\times14\times5$
$= 22 \times 2 \times 14 \times 5 = 3080$m$^3$

questions.question_text: A solid piece of iron is in the form of a cuboids of dimensions (49cm $\times$ 33cm $\times$ 24cm) is melted and moulded to form a solid sphere. The radius of the sphere is
questions.solution:
Let radius of sphere be $r$ cm.
$\therefore$ Volume of sphere $=$ volume of cuboid
$\Rightarrow \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = L \times b \times h$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = 49 \times 33 \times 24$
$\Rightarrow \frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times r^3 = 49 \times 33 \times 24$
$\Rightarrow r^3 = \frac{49 \times 33 \times 24 \times 3 \times 7}{4 \times 22}$
$\Rightarrow r^3 = 9261$
$\therefore r = \sqrt[3]{9261} = 21 \times 21 \times 21 = 21$ cm.

questions.question_text: A patient in a hospital is given soup daily in a cylindrical bowl of diameter 7 cm. If the bowl is filled with soup to a height of 4 cm. How much soup the hospital has to prepare daily to serve 250 patients?
questions.solution:
Diameter of bowl $= 7$cm
$\therefore$ Radius of bowl $= \frac{7}{2}$ cm.
Height $= 4$cm
$\therefore$ Volume of cylindrical bowl $= \pi r^2 h$
$= \frac{22}{7}\times\frac{7}{2}\times\frac{7}{2}\times4 = 154$ cu.cm
Hence, volume of soup for $250$ patients $= 154 \times 250$
$= 38500$ cm$^3 = 38.5$L.

questions.question_text: A sphere and a cube have same surface area. The ratio of squares of their volumes is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of the sphere be $R$ and length of each side of the cube $= a$
Surface area of sphere $= 4\pi R^2$
Surface area of a cube $= 6a^2$
$\Rightarrow 4\pi R^2 = 6a^2$
$\Rightarrow \frac{R^2}{a^2} = \frac{6}{4\pi} = \frac{3}{2\pi}$
Radio of the square of the volumes
$= \left(\frac{\frac{4}{3}\pi R^3}{a^3}\right)^2$
$= \frac{16}{9}\pi^2 \cdot \frac{R^6}{a^6}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{16}{9}\pi^2 \left(\frac{R^2}{a^2}\right)^3$
$= \frac{16}{9}\pi^2 \left(\frac{3}{2\pi}\right)^3$

questions.question_text: The radius of a sphere is equal to the radius of the base of a right circular cone, and the volume of the sphere is double the volume of the cone. The ratio of the height of the cone to the radius of its base is
questions.solution:
Let the radius of cone and the sphere be $R$ and the height of the cone be $H$.
Volume of sphere $= \frac{4}{3}\pi R^3$
Volume of cone $= \frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H$
According to given information $= \frac{4}{3}\pi R^3 = 2 \times \frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H$
$\Rightarrow 4R = 2H$
$\Rightarrow \frac{H}{R} = \frac{4}{2} = 2:1$

questions.question_text: A rectangular paper of 44 cm long and 6 cm wide is rolled to form a cylinder of height equal to width of the paper. The radius of the base of the cylinder so rolled is
questions.solution:
Length of rectangle paper $=$ circumference of the base of cylinder
If $r$ is the radius of the cylinder $44 = 2\pi r$
$\Rightarrow r = \frac{44 \times 7}{2 \times 22} = 7$ cm.

questions.question_text: If three metallic spheres of radii 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm are melted to form a single sphere, then the diameter of the new sphere will be
questions.solution:
Given Raddi of three metallic spheres be $r_1, r_2, r_3$ are $6$cm, $8$cm and $10$cm respectively.
Let the radius of the new sphere be $R$.
$\frac{4}{3}\pi R^3 = \frac{4}{3}\pi (r_1^3 + r_2^3 + r_3^3)$
$\frac{4}{3}\pi R^3 = \frac{4}{3}\pi (6^3 + 8^3 + 10^3)$
$R^3 = (216 + 512 + 1000) = 1728$
$\Rightarrow R = \sqrt[3]{12 \times 12 \times 12}$
$R = 12$
Diameter $= 24$cm

questions.question_text: If the height of a right circular cone is increased by 200% and the radius of the base is reduced by 50%, then the volume of the cone
questions.solution:
Let the radius of a right circular cone be $R$ cm and height be $H$ cm.
Volume of right circular cone $= \frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H$ cu.cm.
When height of right circular cone is increased by $200\%$ and radius of the base is reduced by $50\%$.
New volume $= \frac{1}{3}\pi \left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \cdot 3H$
$= \frac{1}{3}\pi \frac{R^2}{4} \cdot 3H = \frac{1}{4}\pi R^2 H$
Difference $= \pi R^2 H \left(\frac{1}{3} - \frac{1}{4}\right) = \frac{1}{12}\pi R^2 H$
Decrease percentage $= \frac{\frac{1}{12}\pi R^2 H}{\frac{1}{3}\pi R^2 H} \times 100 = 25\%$

questions.question_text: If the radius of a sphere is increased by 10%, then the volume will be increased by
questions.solution:
If $R$ is the radius of sphere, volume of the sphere
$= \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3$.
When radius of sphere is increased by 10%.
New volume $= \frac{4}{3} \pi (1.1R)^3$
$= \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 (1.331)$
Difference $= \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 (1.331) - \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 (1.331 - 1)$
$= \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 (0.331)$
Increase% $= \frac{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 (0.331)}{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3} \times 100 = 33.1\%$

questions.question_text: When a ball bounces, it rises to $\frac{2}{3}$ of the height from which it fell. If the ball is dropped from a height of 36 m, how high will it rise at the third bounce?
questions.solution:
Ball is dropped from the height of 36m when the ball will
rise at the third bounce
Required height $= 36 \times \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2}{3}$
$= \frac{32}{3} = 10 \frac{2}{3}$ m.

questions.question_text: A swimming pool 9m wide and 12m long and 1m deep on the shallow side and 4m deep on the deeper side. Its volume is
questions.solution:
Given length of width of swimming pool is 9m and 12m
respectively.
Volume of swimming pool $= 9 \times 12 \times \left(\frac{1+4}{2}\right)$
$= 9 \times 12 \times \frac{5}{2} = 270$ cu. meter.

questions.question_text: A metal cube of edge 12 cm is melted and formed into three smaller cubes. If the edges of two smaller cubes are 6cm and 8cm, find the edges of the third smaller cube.
questions.solution:
Let Edge of third small cube be $x$ cm
Volume of cube $= (\text{edge})^3$
According to the question, $6^3 + 8^3 + x^3 = 12^3$
$\Rightarrow 216 + 512 + x^3 = 1728$
$x^3 = 1728 - 728 = 1000$
$\Rightarrow x = \sqrt[3]{1000} = 10$ cm
